---
title: "Planning and Designing Useful Evaluations"
description: "Study note on Planning and Designing Useful Evaluations in Handbook of Practical Program Evaluation."
book_id: "handbook-practical-program-evaluation"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Major section"
source_pages: "49-51"
citation_key: "newcomer_hatry_wholey_2015_handbook_practical_program_evaluation"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:ch01-chapter-1-planning-and-designing-useful-evaluations:c0003"
source_status: "needs_review"
---
## Source

<div class="chapter-meta"><span>2015</span><span>MCP evaluation-texts</span><span>Status: needs_review</span><span>Source pages 49-51</span><span>Major section</span></div>
This note summarises the MCP research-library section "Planning and Designing Useful Evaluations" from Kathryn E. Newcomer, Harry P. Hatry, and Joseph S. Wholey's *Handbook of Practical Program Evaluation* [@newcomer_hatry_wholey_2015_handbook_practical_program_evaluation]. It is a paraphrased study note based on source-linked retrieval records, not a substitute for the source text.

## Core Argument

The chapter emphasizes that evaluation should improve programs as well as support accountability.

## Study Summary

The chapter emphasizes that evaluation should improve programs as well as support accountability.

A study that produces defensible findings but no improvement leverage may not be worth its cost.

The evaluator's practical task is to help managers and funders get credible information for decisions they actually face.

## Key Concepts

- Usefulness
- Accountability
- Program improvement
- Decision support

## Source Location

- Section path: Part 1: Evaluation Planning and Design > Chapter 1: Planning and Designing Useful Evaluations
- Evidence records: `chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:ch01-chapter-1-planning-and-designing-useful-evaluations:c0003`

## Practical Implications

- Ask what decision the evaluation will inform before choosing the design.
- Include improvement questions alongside accountability questions where appropriate.
- Report findings in ways managers can use.

## Connections

- Book overview: [Overview](index.qmd)
- Concepts: [Core Concepts](concepts.qmd)
- Map: [Chapter Map](chapter-map.qmd)
- Practice synthesis: [Practice Implications](practice-implications.qmd)

## Study Prompts

1. What is the main evaluation problem that Planning and Designing Useful Evaluations helps diagnose?
2. Which assumptions would need evidence before using this guidance in a live evaluation?
3. How would this change the way an evaluator scopes questions, methods, or reporting?
4. Where could the guidance be misused if treated as a fixed template?

## References
